document.title: Consolidated Product Requirements — TamerLang Feature Roadmap
document.notes: Consolidated from three app-specific PRDs (Duolingo, Babbel, Memrise). 36 source features merged into 18. Duplicates removed; overlapping features harmonised. Out-of-scope items consolidated at the end.

features.name: Spaced Repetition System
features.description: Schedule each word for review at increasing intervals using the SM-2 algorithm. The backend SRS implementation already exists; this feature wires it to the frontend so the quiz loads due cards from GET /srs/due and submits quality ratings (0–5) to POST /srs/review after each answer.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want the app to automatically surface words I need to review today so that I reinforce vocabulary at the optimal moment for long-term retention.
acceptance_criteria: GET /srs/due returns words where nextReview <= now for the authenticated user.
After each quiz answer, POST /srs/review is called with wordId, quality (0–5), and responseTime.
Words answered correctly (quality >= 3) receive an extended interval; incorrect (quality <= 2) reset to a short interval.
A word answered correctly three or more consecutive times is not shown again for at least 7 days.
The review session ends when the due queue is exhausted.
The home screen shows the count of words due for review today.
If no words are due, the UI shows an 'All caught up!' state with time until next review.
backend_changes: Create UserWordProgress schema: { userId, wordId, interval, nextReviewAt, easeFactor, consecutiveCorrect, consecutiveIncorrect, repetitions }.
POST /srs/review: record result and compute next review date via SM-2.
GET /srs/due: return words where nextReviewAt <= now for the authenticated user.
GET /srs/progress: return per-word progress data for the authenticated user.
frontend_changes: Refactor VocabularyProvider to fetch from GET /srs/due via authFetch instead of localStorage.
Add review submission call inside useTimedQuizMachine after SELECT_ANSWER and TIMEOUT actions.
Add 'due today' count badge to the home page.
Add loading and error states to VocabularyProvider.
Add a dedicated /review route.

features.name: Difficult Words Queue
features.description: Surface words with two or more consecutive incorrect answers into a dedicated review session available at any time, independent of the SRS schedule.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want a dedicated session for my most problematic words so that I can focus extra effort where it is needed most.
acceptance_criteria: Words answered incorrectly twice in a row appear in the Difficult Words list.
Answering a difficult word correctly twice in succession removes it from the list.
The difficult word count is shown on the home/dashboard screen.
A 'Difficult Words' mode is selectable on the quiz start screen.
Difficult words feed into the existing quiz machine with no new UI components required.
backend_changes: consecutiveIncorrect counter is part of UserWordProgress (defined in F-01).
GET /srs/difficult: return words where consecutiveIncorrect >= 2 for the authenticated user.
frontend_changes: Add 'Difficult Words' mode option on the quiz start screen.
Show difficult word count on the home/dashboard screen.

features.name: Flashcard Mode
features.description: A flip-card exercise where the learner sees the Uzbek word, taps to reveal the translation, and self-rates recall ('Got it' / 'Missed it'). Maps to SM-2 quality 5 or 1. Lower-pressure than timed multiple-choice; useful for learning new vocabulary before formal review.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want a low-pressure flashcard mode so that I can self-assess my recall without the stress of a timed quiz.
acceptance_criteria: A questionType field is added to the quiz state machine supporting 'multiple-choice' and 'flashcard' values.
Flashcard view shows the Uzbek word on the front; tapping reveals the translation.
Two action buttons: 'Got it' (submits quality 5) and 'Missed it' (submits quality 1).
Ratings are submitted to POST /srs/review at the end of the session or per-card.
Flashcard mode is accessible from the /review route as an alternative to multiple-choice.
frontend_changes: Add 'flashcard' value to questionType in useTimedQuizMachine.
New FlashCard component with flip animation and 'Got it' / 'Missed it' buttons.
Mode selector on the quiz/review start screen.

features.name: Typing and Fill-in-the-Blank Exercise
features.description: Two related active-recall formats: (1) Typing Practice — the word is shown and the learner types the translation; (2) Fill-in-the-Blank — an example sentence is shown with one word blanked and the learner types the missing Uzbek word. Both use case-insensitive fuzzy matching. Fill-in-the-Blank requires example sentences (F-12).
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to type answers rather than always selecting from a list so that I build active recall and spelling skills.
acceptance_criteria: A questionType 'typing' is added to the quiz state machine.
Typing Practice: the word is shown; learner types the translation; validation is case-insensitive with trimmed whitespace.
Fuzzy matching accepts minor typos (edit distance <= 1 for words up to 6 chars, <= 2 for longer).
Fill-in-the-Blank questions only appear for words that have an exampleSentence (F-12).
On incorrect answer, the correct word is revealed with the full sentence.
Correct answer maps to SM-2 quality 4; incorrect to quality 1.
exerciseType is submitted to POST /srs/review to distinguish formats.
backend_changes: Add exerciseType field to POST /srs/review payload: multiple_choice | flashcard | typing | fill_in_the_blank | matching.
frontend_changes: New TypingExercise component with text input and fuzzy-match utility.
New FillInTheBlank component rendering example sentence with blanked word.
Add 'typing' and 'fill-in-the-blank' values to questionType in useTimedQuizMachine.
Mode selector on the quiz start screen.

features.name: Matching Pairs Exercise
features.description: A grid of Uzbek words and English translations shown in shuffled order. The learner taps a word then its matching translation; matched pairs disappear. Designed as a lesson-introduction mode to associate a batch of new words before formal SRS review.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want a matching exercise so that I can associate a batch of new words with their translations in an active, game-like way before starting timed review.
acceptance_criteria: Matching exercise is available as a lesson-introduction mode, not a primary SRS review mode.
Grids contain 4–6 pairs drawn from the current lesson's words.
Correct matches are visually confirmed and removed from the grid.
Incorrect matches flash red and remain on the grid.
Completing the exercise triggers SRS enrolment for matched words (nextReview = tomorrow).
Results are submitted to POST /srs/review with exerciseType = 'matching'.
frontend_changes: New MatchingPairs component.
Integrate with lesson start flow (F-09).

features.name: Mistake Review Mode
features.description: After a quiz session the results screen offers a 'Review Mistakes' button that re-runs only the words the user answered incorrectly. The session is unscored so it serves as low-stakes reinforcement while the session is fresh.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to immediately re-practice words I got wrong so that I reinforce correct answers while the session is fresh.
acceptance_criteria: The quiz results screen lists all words answered incorrectly with their correct translations.
A 'Review Mistakes' button is enabled only when at least one answer was wrong.
Clicking it starts a new quiz session pre-loaded with only the incorrect words.
The mistake-review session does not submit results to POST /srs/review.
After the mistake-review session the user is returned to the results screen.
frontend_changes: Results screen lists incorrect answers with correct translations.
Add 'Review Mistakes' button that reinitialises useTimedQuizMachine with the filtered word list.
Pass an unscored flag to suppress review submission in mistake-review mode.

features.name: Streak Tracking
features.description: Track consecutive calendar days on which the user completes at least one review session. Reset to 0 on any missed day. Display current streak and longest streak prominently. A streak-shield mechanic (prevents one reset) is deferred to V2.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to see my consecutive days of study so that I have a concrete, daily reason to open the app.
acceptance_criteria: User schema gains: currentStreak (number), longestStreak (number), lastActivityDate (Date).
POST /srs/review increments currentStreak if lastActivityDate was yesterday; resets to 1 if more than 1 day has passed; no-ops if already updated today.
longestStreak is updated whenever currentStreak exceeds it.
The home page and nav bar display the current streak with a flame icon.
Missing a calendar day resets currentStreak to 0 (no streak-freeze mechanic in V1).
A streak milestone animation plays when the streak increments.
backend_changes: Add currentStreak, longestStreak, lastActivityDate to User schema.
Update streak counters inside the POST /srs/review handler.
Expose streak values via GET /users/me.
frontend_changes: Display streak flame icon + counter in the nav bar or home screen.
Show streak milestone animation on increment.

features.name: Learning Goals
features.description: Learners set a daily target (number of words reviewed) and a weekly session-count target. Progress toward both is shown on the home screen. Daily goal resets at midnight; weekly goal resets every Monday. No push notifications in V1 (see F-14).
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to set daily and weekly learning targets so that I have clear, achievable goals without feeling that a single missed day ruins everything.
acceptance_criteria: User schema gains: dailyGoal (number, default 10), weeklyGoal (number, default 5), weeklySessionsCompleted (number), weeklyGoalResetDate (Date).
PATCH /users/me/goals accepts { dailyGoal, weeklyGoal } with validation (dailyGoal 1–50, weeklyGoal 1–30).
POST /srs/review increments weeklySessionsCompleted and resets it at the start of each new week.
Home screen shows a daily progress bar (words reviewed today vs dailyGoal) and a weekly progress ring (sessions this week vs weeklyGoal).
Reaching the daily goal triggers a congratulatory indicator.
Goal-setting is included in onboarding (F-16) and editable from user settings.
backend_changes: Add dailyGoal, weeklyGoal, weeklySessionsCompleted, weeklyGoalResetDate to User schema.
PATCH /users/me/goals endpoint.
frontend_changes: Daily progress bar on home/dashboard.
Weekly progress ring on home/dashboard.
Goal picker in onboarding flow and user settings.

features.name: Lesson and Unit Structure
features.description: Words are grouped into named Lessons (e.g. 'Greetings', 'Numbers 1–20') organised into Topics. A skill-map grid shows completion status. Starting a lesson enrolls its words in SRS. A lesson is complete when all its words reach the Strong tier. Prerequisite lessons must be completed before the next unlocks.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want vocabulary organised into thematic lessons so that I can study related words together and have a clear sense of where I am in the curriculum.
acceptance_criteria: Lesson schema: { id, title, topic, language, wordIds: UUID[], order: number, prerequisiteLessonIds: UUID[], iconUrl?: string }.
GET /lessons returns all lessons for a language ordered by topic and lesson order.
POST /lessons/:id/start enrolls all lesson words in SRS (creates UserWordProgress records with nextReview = now).
The frontend shows a skill-map grid with per-lesson status: Not Started / In Progress / Complete.
A lesson is Complete when all its words have interval >= 7 days.
A lesson is locked until all its prerequisites are Complete.
Clicking an unlocked new lesson opens Matching Pairs intro (F-05); in-progress lessons go straight to filtered review.
Word schema gains optional lessonId and topic fields.
backend_changes: Create Lesson schema with title, topic, language, wordIds, order, prerequisiteLessonIds, iconUrl.
Create LessonsModule with CRUD endpoints (admin-only writes) and GET /lessons.
POST /lessons/:id/start endpoint.
GET /lessons/:id/progress: per-user mastery % and completion status.
Add lessonId and topic fields to Word schema.
frontend_changes: New /lessons page with skill-map grid.
LessonCard component: icon, title, completion bar, locked/unlocked state.
Clicking a card launches Matching Pairs intro (new lesson) or filtered review (in-progress).

features.name: Progress Dashboard & Statistics
features.description: A personal stats page aggregating the learner's history: vocabulary by strength tier (Weak / Medium / Strong), current and longest streak, daily activity heatmap, accuracy over 7 and 30 days, lessons progress, and total reviews. All data derives from existing SRS and streak fields.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want a single page that shows my overall learning progress so that I can feel a sense of accomplishment and identify what to focus on next.
acceptance_criteria: Strength tiers — Weak: interval <= 1 day or repetitions < 2; Medium: interval 2–7 days; Strong: interval > 7 days.
GET /srs/stats returns: wordsByTier {weak, medium, strong}, totalReviews, accuracyAllTime, accuracy7Days, accuracy30Days.
GET /users/me/stats returns: currentStreak, longestStreak, totalSessions, lessonsStarted, lessonsCompleted.
Dashboard shows: streak, word-tier chart, accuracy percentages, 7-day activity bar chart, 90-day activity heatmap, lesson progress.
Page loads within 2 seconds on a cold session.
Dashboard is accessible from the main navigation.
Data reflects the authenticated user's history only.
backend_changes: GET /srs/stats endpoint: aggregate tier counts, accuracy metrics.
GET /users/me/stats endpoint: streak, session, and lesson aggregates.
Store per-session summary (date, correctCount, incorrectCount) in a Sessions collection.
frontend_changes: New /dashboard page linked from nav.
StatsCard components for streak, word counts by tier, accuracy.
Bar or donut chart for word-tier distribution.
7-day activity bar chart.
90-day activity heatmap component.

features.name: Audio Pronunciation
features.description: Attach an audio clip (CDN URL) to each word so learners hear correct Uzbek pronunciation. Audio plays automatically on word reveal; a speaker icon allows replay on demand. TTS synthesis is acceptable in V1; native recordings preferred for V2. An optional listening-mode exercise (hear audio, pick the word) extends this feature.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to hear Uzbek words pronounced correctly so that I build accurate pronunciation habits from the start.
acceptance_criteria: Word schema gains optional audioUrl (string) field.
Audio plays automatically when a word is shown in flashcard or answer-reveal states (configurable per user).
A speaker icon appears on any word display when audioUrl is present; tapping it replays the audio.
Audio is gracefully skipped with no error shown when audioUrl is null.
Admin tooling or a seed script can bulk-update audioUrl values.
Optional: a listening mode plays audio first and asks the learner to select the matching written word.
backend_changes: Add audioUrl (string, optional) to Word schema; include in all word query responses.
Support audio URL association via PATCH /words/:id.
frontend_changes: New AudioPlayer component wrapping the HTML5 Audio API.
Render AudioPlayer in Translation and FlashCard components when audioUrl is present.
Add 'auto-play audio' toggle to user settings.
Optional: ListenAndChoose exercise type component.

features.name: Example Sentences
features.description: Add optional bilingual example sentences (Uzbek + English) to each word. Shown during the quiz answer-reveal phase to give context. Also powers the Fill-in-the-Blank exercise (F-04).
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to see a word used in a sentence so that I understand its meaning and usage beyond a bare translation.
acceptance_criteria: Word schema gains optional exampleSentences: { uz: string, en: string }[] field.
During answer-reveal, one example sentence is displayed below the translation when available.
Sentence is styled distinctly (smaller font, italic).
Words without example sentences show nothing extra (no empty placeholder).
exampleSentences is included in all word query responses.
backend_changes: Add exampleSentences: { uz: string, en: string }[] (optional) to Word schema and DTOs.
Include exampleSentences in all word query responses.
frontend_changes: Render one exampleSentences entry in the answer-reveal phase of TimedVocabularyTest.
Style distinctly from the main translation (smaller, italic).

features.name: Achievement Badges
features.description: One-time awards for reaching learning milestones, evaluated server-side after each review submission. A toast notification appears in the UI when a new badge is earned. Badges are displayed on the user profile page.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to earn badges for meaningful milestones so that my progress is marked with discrete moments of celebration.
acceptance_criteria: User schema gains earnedBadges: { id: string, awardedAt: Date }[].
Initial badge set: FirstReview, SevenStreak, ThirtyStreak, TenWordsStrong, FiftyWordsLearned, FiveHundredReviews, FirstLessonCompleted.
Badge conditions are evaluated server-side after POST /srs/review.
Newly awarded badges are returned in the POST /srs/review response as newlyEarnedBadges.
Frontend shows a badge unlock animation/toast when new badges are returned.
Profile page lists all earned badges with icons, names, and award dates.
backend_changes: Add earnedBadges: { id, awardedAt }[] to User schema.
Create badge definitions map (id, name, description, condition function).
Check and award badges inside POST /srs/review handler.
Include newlyEarnedBadges in the review response payload.
frontend_changes: Toast notification triggered by newlyEarnedBadges in review response.
Badges section on profile/dashboard page.

features.name: Daily Reminder Notifications
features.description: Send a browser push notification or email at a user-configured time on days the user has not yet met their daily goal. Notification includes the user's current streak count and a deep link to the review screen.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want a daily reminder if I have not studied yet so that I do not break my streak by forgetting.
acceptance_criteria: Users can set a reminder time (HH:MM local time) and toggle reminders on/off in settings.
A daily cron job identifies users with reminders enabled who have not yet met their daily goal.
Notifications are sent via web push or transactional email.
Notification body includes the user's current streak count and a link to the app.
No notification is sent on days the daily goal is already met.
Disabling reminders stops all future notifications immediately.
backend_changes: Add notificationEnabled (boolean), notificationTime (string HH:MM), pushSubscription (object) to User schema.
Integrate a push library (e.g. web-push) or email service (e.g. Resend).
Add a cron job via @nestjs/schedule that fires at each unique reminder time.
frontend_changes: Notification settings section in user settings page.
Register service worker and request push permission on opt-in.
Save push subscription object via PATCH /users/me.

features.name: Experience Points & Leaderboards
features.description: Award XP for each review answer (correct: 10 XP, incorrect: 4 XP for participation). XP accumulates weekly and drives a leaderboard ranking among followed users plus an optional global top-10. Weekly XP resets every Monday at 00:00 UTC. Note: Babbel intentionally omits social leaderboards; they are included here at low priority based on Duolingo and Memrise precedent.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to see how my weekly XP compares to friends so that friendly competition keeps me engaged.
acceptance_criteria: Correct answers award 10 XP; incorrect answers award 4 XP.
User schema gains weeklyXp (number), totalXp (number); weeklyXp resets via a Monday cron job.
Users can follow/unfollow other users by user ID or username.
Leaderboard page shows weekly XP for the current user and all followed users, ranked descending.
Optional global top-10 section shows highest-ranked users this week.
Leaderboard data is cached and refreshed at most every 5 minutes.
If the user follows nobody, the page shows a prompt to find friends.
User can see their own rank even if outside the visible list.
backend_changes: Add weeklyXp, totalXp to User schema; add xpEarned to UserWordProgress review events.
Add following: string[] (user IDs) to User schema.
POST /users/follow/:id and DELETE /users/follow/:id endpoints.
GET /leaderboard/weekly: ranked list of followed users + self with weeklyXp.
GET /srs/xp?period=today|week|month aggregation endpoint.
Weekly cron to reset weeklyXp.
frontend_changes: New /leaderboard page.
LeaderboardRow component: rank, avatar, name, weekly XP bar.
Follow/unfollow button on user profile pages.
XP counter in header or dashboard.

features.name: Onboarding Intake Flow
features.description: A 3-screen onboarding flow shown to new users after first login. Collects reason for learning (heritage, travel, work, curiosity), self-assessed proficiency level (beginner, elementary, intermediate), and learning goals. Responses are stored on the User record and used to recommend a starting lesson and pre-populate goal defaults.
features.user_story: As a new user, I want to tell the app why I am learning and how much I already know so that I start at the right level instead of from absolute zero.
acceptance_criteria: User schema gains: learningGoal (enum), proficiencyLevel (enum: beginner|elementary|intermediate), onboardingCompleted (boolean).
Onboarding flow is shown once on first login when onboardingCompleted === false.
PATCH /users/me/onboarding saves collected fields and sets onboardingCompleted = true.
Proficiency level influences which lessons appear first on the lesson map (F-09).
Goals set during onboarding pre-populate Learning Goals defaults (F-08).
Users can re-run onboarding from settings to update preferences.
backend_changes: Add learningGoal, proficiencyLevel, onboardingCompleted to User schema.
PATCH /users/me/onboarding endpoint.
frontend_changes: New /onboarding multi-step page.
Redirect to /onboarding after first login when onboardingCompleted === false.
Feed collected goal values into F-08 goal-setting defaults.

features.name: User-Created Mnemonics
features.description: Allow users to attach a personal text or image mnemonic to any word to aid memorisation. Other users can upvote mnemonics. The highest-voted mnemonic for a word is shown during the answer-reveal phase.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to attach a personal memory trick to a word so that I can recall it more easily during review.
acceptance_criteria: Any authenticated user can create a mnemonic for any word.
Mnemonic schema: { id, wordId, authorId, text, imageUrl?, upvotes: number }.
The mnemonic with the most upvotes is displayed by default during answer-reveal.
Users cannot upvote their own mnemonic.
A mnemonic creation form is accessible from the word detail view.
backend_changes: New Mnemonic schema: { id, wordId, authorId, text, imageUrl?, upvotes: number }.
GET and POST /words/:id/mnemonics endpoints.
POST /words/:id/mnemonics/:mnemonicId/upvote endpoint.
frontend_changes: Mnemonic display during answer-reveal.
Mnemonic creation form in word detail view.
Upvote button on each mnemonic.

features.name: Offline Mode
features.description: Keep the quiz fully functional without network connectivity by caching the app shell and word data via a service worker. Queue SRS result submissions in IndexedDB and flush them automatically when connectivity is restored.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want the app to work without an internet connection so that I can study during a commute or in areas with poor coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Quiz runs without any network requests once words are loaded.
Service worker caches the app shell and most-recently-fetched word data.
Unsynchronised progress events are stored in IndexedDB and flushed automatically on reconnect.
A clear offline indicator is shown in the UI when the app is offline.
All queued events are flushed before fresh due-word data is fetched after reconnect.
frontend_changes: Service worker (or next-pwa) to cache app shell and word data.
IndexedDB queue for unsynchronised progress events.
Flush queue on reconnect before fetching fresh due words.
Offline indicator component.

out_of_scope.reason: Operationally complex, high cost, not viable for a small team. Babbel also discontinued this for consumer use in 2025.
High infrastructure cost; mature Uzbek NLP models do not yet exist.
Requires significant Uzbek audio content production; addressable once the core learning loop is stable.
Premature given current word count; the lesson/topic structure in F-09 is sufficient for this stage.
Scope is vocabulary acquisition; grammar instruction is a separate product surface.
